content:
99% of people are having the wrong conversation

5 min read
Just now

--

I hate the current conversation about AI writing.
Most people fall into one of two camps.
Either using AI to write is totally ok and smart (if you can trick people into thinking it’s “human”) or all AI writing is bad.
Both are wrong.
The reality is that some things can be 100% AI written, and other things should be 0%.
Note: If you don’t have a paid Medium account, you can read for free here.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Let’s start with something that no one would care whether AI wrote it or not.
Imagine you bought some Ikea furniture. Do you care if the instructions were written by a human or with AI?
Of course not.
All you would care about is whether the instructions are good, clear, and easy to follow.
Now I’ll talk about the other end of the spectrum.
A couple of years ago, I read the book: 438 Days: An Extraordinary True Story of Survival at Sea
It is based on the incredible true story of José Salvador Alvarenga, a fisherman from the coast of southern Mexico who was swept far into the Pacific Ocean after a violent storm pushed his small…
